Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|qid=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Matsuyama|Q200022|qid=Q200022|1=Matsuyama}}Hattachi 106, 107 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Amaterukuniteruhikoame-no-hoakari-kushitama-nigihayahi-no-Mikoto) |社格={{ill|lt=Shikinaisha (small)|Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}}, former {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}} |本殿= {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|qid=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |例祭=First and second day of the October three-day holiday |地図= {{Location map|Japan Ehime|label=Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto
Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine''' (くにつひこのみことじんじゃ) is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} in Hattachi, {{ill|lt=Matsuyama|Q200022|qid=Q200022|1=Matsuyama}} (former {{ill|lt=Hōjō|Q94789|qid=Q94789|1=Hōjō, Ehime}}), {{ill|lt=Ehime Prefecture|Q123376|qid=Q123376|1=Ehime Prefecture}}. Its former rank was {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=kensha|1=Ken-sha|qid=Q90139226}}. It stands atop a {{ill|lt=kofun|Q1141225|qid=Q1141225|1=Kofun}} (Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine Kofun; total length 50 m). === Overview === The enshrined deity is {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}. He is also called Amaterukuniteruhikoame-no-hoakari-kushitama-nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. Although it is called Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine, the deity is an {{ill|lt=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami|Q11442793|qid=Q11442793|lt=Amatsukami|1=Amatsukami and Kunitsukami}}. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Umashimaji-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}, Mononobe no Asari no Mikoto, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q317997|lt=Hondawake-no-Mikoto|1=Emperor Ōjin|qid=Q317997}} are also enshrined. It faces {{ill|lt=Kushitama-hime-no-Mikoto Shrine|Q134888243|qid=Q134888243|1=Kushitamahime Shrine}}. Locally, the two shrines together are called "Kazahaya-no-Miya Ōujigami." 画像:Kunitsuhikonomikoto-jinja 20240523 (1).jpg|Front entrance 画像:Kunitsuhikonomikoto-jinja 20240912 (1).jpg|Rear approach 画像:Kunitsuhikonomikoto-jinja 20240523 (4).jpg|Stone steps where mikoshi dropping is performed 画像:Kunitsuhikonomikoto-jinja 20240523 (6).jpg|Rōmon gate (Ehime Prefecture designated tangible cultural property) 画像:Kunitsuhikonomikoto-jinja 20240912 (3).jpg|Kagura hall 画像:Kunitsuhikonomikoto-jinja 20240523 (7).jpg|Stone steps leading to the shrine buildings === Precinct shrines === *Kotohira-gū (deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}}) *Inari-sha (deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Inari Ōkami|1=UNKNOWN}}) 画像:Kunitsuhikonomikoto-jinja 20240912 (4).jpg|Kotohira-gū 画像:Kunitsuhikonomikoto-jinja 20241107 (2).jpg|Inari-sha === Annual festival === On the first day of the October three-day holiday, the Godōza Festival is held; on the second day, the Shinkō Festival is held. Called the Kazahaya Fire Festival, it includes throwing a mikoshi down stone steps, and is one of Japan's three major "rough mikoshi" festivalsThe other two are Miyagi's "Hote Matsuri" and Himeji's "Nada no Kenka Matsuri".. === Cultural properties === *Rōmon gate (Ehime Prefecture designated tangible cultural property)[https://www.city.matsuyama.ehime.jp/kanko/kankoguide/rekishibunka/bunkazai/ken/kunituhiko_roumon.html Matsuyama City website] === See also === *{{ill|lt=List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province|Q11378813|qid=Q11378813|1=List of Shikinaisha in Iyo Province}} *{{ill|lt=Kazahaya District|Q6380771|qid=Q6380771|1=Kazahaya District, Ehime}} === Notes === {{reflist}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Kunitsuhiko-no-Mikoto Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Matsuyama]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Iyo Province]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Ehime Prefecture]] [[Category:松山市の建築物]] [[Category:松山市の歴史]] [[Category:Iyo Province]] [[Category:Ehime Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]]
